prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿高抬腿跳" (single-leg high-knee jump) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the peak of a single-leg high-knee jump: right leg fully extended pushing off the ground (toes about to leave or just left the ground); left thigh driven up high to hip height or above with knee bent at 90 degrees; arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; explosive single-leg jump posture; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the jumping (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the lifted leg and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿 / 股四头肌" pointing to the calf and quadriceps of the jumping leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "髂腰肌 / 核心" pointing to the hip flexors and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
